element_introduction_order.element: Basic terrain and plot of land
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with free plot in United Kingdom with initial capital
Primary revenue sources; player learns price-setting and customer management
Low-complexity attractions; introduce ride placement and maintenance concepts
Require track layout; higher cost and complexity; unlock via research
Lack of staff causes cascading problems (messy paths, breakdowns, crime); teaches resource allocation
Unlocks new rides, shops, and facility improvements; requires ongoing player allocation
Neglected rides explode; teaches upkeep necessity
Unhappy visitors attract thugs who damage park; teaches service quality feedback loop
Failed negotiations trigger strikes or supply loss; introduces economic pressure
Available early but requires understanding of cash flow
New parks have different economy, weather, terrain, land value; requires adaptive strategy
Entertainment additions; some platforms have platform-specific variants

typical_relief_triggers: Successful ride placement and visitor arrivals
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Calendar-based annual cycles with adjustable game speed; tension builds throughout year toward end-of-year evaluation; management crises create punctuated difficulty spikes within cycles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Research system gates attractions; player chooses which rides/shops to unlock based on strategy

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic shops and simple rides; teaches revenue mechanics and staff placement
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complexity escalates with advanced rides requiring track layout, staff management becomes critical, maintenance demands increase, visitor happiness affects park quality
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full economic simulation with negotiations, stock management, research decisions; world locations introduce environmental variables; multiple rival parks create competitive pressure
notable_spikes: Introduction of staff shortages causing cascading failures
Transitions to new world locations with unfamiliar economies and terrain

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divides progression into calendar years; end-of-year evaluation against rivals provides checkpoint tension; player can continue or move to new location
Player allocates research funding to gradually unlock attractions, improving existing rides, increasing bus capacity, and enhancing staff efficiency; creates long-term planning tension
Different world locations feature varying economies, weather, terrain, and land values; requires strategy adjustment; Mega Drive/SNES versions feature visual theme variations (desert, glacier)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Geographic world locations available after initial park sale

notable_level_patterns.description: Single problems (staff shortage, ride breakdown, vandalism) trigger secondary failures (messy paths, visitor unhappiness, crime); teaches interconnected system thinking
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game park experiencing multiple simultaneous failures
